condition_keywords: osteoarthritis;arthritis;joint pain;hip dysplasia;postoperative pain;post surgical pain
indication: Carprofen (Rimadyl) is labeled for control of pain and inflammation associated with osteoarthritis and for control of postoperative pain following soft tissue and orthopedic surgery in dogs.
guardrail: NSAID: do not combine with other NSAIDs or steroids; requires baseline bloodwork and periodic monitoring; confirm dosing and suitability with your veterinarian.

indication: Meloxicam is labeled for control of pain and inflammation associated with osteoarthritis in dogs, and for control of postoperative pain and inflammation following surgery in cats as a single dose.
guardrail: NSAID: repeated dosing in cats carries added kidney risk and label restrictions differ by species; confirm dosing and suitability with your veterinarian.

indication: Galliprant is labeled for the control of pain and inflammation associated with osteoarthritis in dogs.
guardrail: NSAID (piprant class): not labeled for cats; confirm dosing and suitability with your veterinarian.

indication: Deracoxib (Deramaxx) is labeled for control of postoperative pain and inflammation, and for control of pain and inflammation associated with osteoarthritis, in dogs.
guardrail: NSAID: do not combine with other NSAIDs or steroids; confirm dosing and suitability with your veterinarian.

indication: Firocoxib (Previcox) is labeled for control of pain and inflammation associated with osteoarthritis, and for control of postoperative pain associated with orthopedic surgery, in dogs.
guardrail: NSAID: not labeled for cats; confirm dosing and suitability with your veterinarian.

indication: Robenacoxib (Onsior) is labeled for the control of postoperative pain and inflammation associated with soft tissue surgery, orthopedic surgery, or dental procedures in cats, for a short course.
guardrail: NSAID: labeled only for short-duration use in cats; confirm dosing and suitability with your veterinarian.

indication: Apoquel is labeled for the control of pruritus associated with allergic dermatitis and the control of atopic dermatitis in dogs.
guardrail: Not labeled for dogs under 12 months old or for use in cats; confirm suitability with your veterinarian.

indication: Cytopoint is labeled for the reduction of clinical signs associated with atopic dermatitis, including pruritus, in dogs.
guardrail: Given as a veterinary-administered injection; confirm suitability and timing with your veterinarian.

indication: Atopica (cyclosporine) is labeled for the control of atopic dermatitis in dogs and cats.
guardrail: Suppresses the immune system; requires veterinary monitoring for infection risk; confirm dosing with your veterinarian.

label_status: Extra-label (human-labeled drug) used under veterinary direction
indication: Diphenhydramine is commonly used under veterinary direction for mild allergic reactions, hives, and itching in dogs and cats, though it is labeled for human use, not for animals.
guardrail: Confirm dosing with your veterinarian; some combination human products contain other ingredients, like decongestants, that are unsafe for pets.

indication: Zenrelia is labeled for the control of pruritus associated with allergic dermatitis and the control of atopic dermatitis in dogs.
guardrail: Confirm suitability with your veterinarian, including screening for infection risk before starting.

label_status: Extra-label (human-labeled drug) used under veterinary direction
indication: Famotidine is commonly used under veterinary direction to reduce stomach acid production for gastritis, acid reflux, and mild GI upset in dogs and cats, though it is labeled for human use, not for animals.
guardrail: Confirm dosing with your veterinarian, especially alongside other GI medications.

label_status: Extra-label in dogs and cats; FDA-approved veterinary label in horses (GastroGard/Ulcergard)
indication: Omeprazole (GastroGard/Ulcergard) is labeled for treatment and prevention of gastric ulcers in horses; in dogs and cats it is commonly used under veterinary direction to reduce stomach acid for similar conditions.
guardrail: Confirm dosing and species-specific product with your veterinarian.

indication: Metronidazole is labeled for the treatment of GI infections caused by susceptible anaerobic bacteria and protozoa such as Giardia, in dogs and cats.
guardrail: Complete the full course as directed; confirm dosing with your veterinarian.

indication: Cerenia (maropitant) is labeled for the prevention and treatment of vomiting, and, in dogs, for control of visceral pain.
guardrail: Confirm dosing with your veterinarian; not labeled for animals younger than the label's minimum age.

label_status: Extra-label (human-labeled drug) used under veterinary direction
indication: Sucralfate is commonly used under veterinary direction to coat and protect GI ulcers in dogs and cats, though it is labeled for human use, not for animals.
guardrail: Give separately from other oral medications, as directed by your veterinarian, since it can interfere with their absorption.

indication: Furosemide is labeled for the management of fluid buildup, or edema, associated with congestive heart failure in dogs and cats.
guardrail: Requires monitoring of kidney values and electrolytes; confirm dosing with your veterinarian.

indication: Enalapril is labeled for the management of signs of congestive heart failure in dogs and cats.
guardrail: Requires monitoring of kidney values and blood pressure; confirm dosing with your veterinarian.

condition_keywords: congestive heart failure;dilated cardiomyopathy;heart valve disease
indication: Vetmedin (pimobendan) is labeled for the management of congestive heart failure in dogs caused by dilated cardiomyopathy or valvular heart disease.
guardrail: Confirm dosing and cardiac diagnosis with your veterinarian, ideally alongside an echocardiogram.

label_status: Extra-label (human-labeled drug) used under veterinary direction
indication: Spironolactone is commonly used under veterinary direction as an add-on diuretic for management of congestive heart failure in dogs and cats, though it is labeled for human use, not for animals.
guardrail: Requires monitoring of electrolytes; confirm dosing with your veterinarian.

indication: Phenobarbital is labeled for the control of seizures in dogs and cats.
guardrail: Requires periodic bloodwork to monitor liver function and drug levels; confirm dosing with your veterinarian.

label_status: Extra-label (human-labeled drug) used under veterinary direction
indication: Levetiracetam is commonly used under veterinary direction as an anticonvulsant for seizure control in dogs and cats, though it is labeled for human use, not for animals.
guardrail: Often used alongside other anticonvulsants; confirm dosing with your veterinarian.

label_status: Extra-label (compounded) drug used under veterinary direction
indication: Potassium bromide is commonly used, often compounded, as an add-on anticonvulsant for seizure control in dogs, typically alongside phenobarbital.
guardrail: Requires periodic bloodwork to monitor drug levels; confirm dosing with your veterinarian; use with caution in dogs with kidney disease.

condition_keywords: bacterial infection;skin infection;urinary tract infection;wound infection
indication: Amoxicillin is labeled for the treatment of susceptible bacterial infections, including skin, soft tissue, and urinary tract infections, in dogs and cats.
guardrail: Complete the full course as directed; confirm dosing with your veterinarian.

condition_keywords: bacterial infection;skin infection;urinary tract infection;wound infection;dental infection
indication: Clavamox is labeled for the treatment of skin and soft tissue infections and other susceptible bacterial infections, including those caused by beta-lactamase producing bacteria, in dogs and cats.
guardrail: Complete the full course as directed; confirm dosing with your veterinarian.

condition_keywords: tick borne disease;lyme disease;ehrlichia;bacterial infection;respiratory infection
indication: Doxycycline is labeled and commonly used for the treatment of susceptible bacterial infections, including tick-borne diseases such as Lyme disease and ehrlichiosis, in dogs and cats.
guardrail: Complete the full course as directed; confirm dosing with your veterinarian; give with food to reduce GI upset.

indication: Clindamycin is labeled for the treatment of skin and soft tissue infections, dental and periodontal infections, and bone infections caused by susceptible bacteria, in dogs and cats.
guardrail: Complete the full course as directed; confirm dosing with your veterinarian.

condition_keywords: urinary tract infection;skin infection;respiratory infection;bacterial infection
indication: Enrofloxacin (Baytril) is labeled for the treatment of susceptible bacterial infections, including urinary tract, skin, and respiratory infections, in dogs and cats.
guardrail: Not labeled for young, growing animals due to joint cartilage risk; confirm dosing with your veterinarian.

indication: Heartgard Plus is labeled for the prevention of heartworm disease and the treatment and control of roundworm and hookworm infections in dogs.
guardrail: Confirm dosing by weight with your veterinarian; monthly prevention should not be missed or delayed.

condition_keywords: flea;tick;flea infestation;tick infestation;flea and tick prevention
indication: NexGard is labeled for the treatment and prevention of flea infestations and the treatment and control of tick infestations in dogs.
guardrail: Confirm dosing by weight with your veterinarian.

condition_keywords: flea;tick;flea infestation;tick infestation;flea and tick prevention
indication: Bravecto is labeled for the treatment and prevention of flea infestations and the treatment and control of tick infestations in dogs and cats, with extended dosing intervals.
guardrail: Confirm dosing by weight and species-specific product with your veterinarian.

indication: Drontal Plus is labeled for the treatment and control of tapeworm, roundworm, and hookworm infections in dogs.
guardrail: Confirm dosing by weight with your veterinarian.

label_status: Extra-label (human-labeled drug) used under veterinary direction
condition_keywords: situational anxiety;travel anxiety;post surgical stress;fear;noise phobia
indication: Trazodone is commonly used under veterinary direction for situational anxiety, such as travel, vet visits, or post-surgical confinement, in dogs, though it is labeled for human use, not for animals.
guardrail: Often used alongside behavior modification; confirm dosing with your veterinarian.

indication: Reconcile (fluoxetine) is labeled for the treatment of separation anxiety in dogs, in conjunction with a behavior modification plan.
guardrail: Effects build over several weeks; confirm dosing with your veterinarian.

indication: Clomicalm (clomipramine) is labeled for the treatment of separation anxiety in dogs, used together with a behavior modification plan.
guardrail: Effects build over several weeks; confirm dosing with your veterinarian.

indication: Methimazole is labeled for the treatment of hyperthyroidism in cats.
guardrail: Requires periodic bloodwork to monitor thyroid levels and blood counts; confirm dosing with your veterinarian.

indication: Vetoryl (trilostane) is labeled for the treatment of pituitary-dependent and adrenal-dependent hyperadrenocorticism, also known as Cushing's disease, in dogs.
guardrail: Requires periodic bloodwork, such as ACTH stimulation testing, to monitor adrenal response; confirm dosing with your veterinarian.

indication: Vetsulin is labeled for the control of blood glucose levels in dogs and cats with diabetes mellitus.
guardrail: Requires careful weight-based dosing and monitoring for hypoglycemia; confirm dosing with your veterinarian.
